content.full_text:
air Political Practices CommissioIL 
P,O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO,95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
107 SOUTH BROADWAY 
• •• 
SUITE 2140A 
••• 
LOS ANGELES,90012 
January 13, 1977 
The Honorable Raymond L. Sullivan 
Associate Justice of the Supreme 
Court of California 
State Building 
San Francisco, California 94102 
Dear Justice Sullivan: 
Thank you for your letter of January 3, 1977 in which you 
request advice regarding filing your Statement of Economic 
Interests. 
In your letter you indicate that you will retire as an 
Associate Justice of the Supreme Court on January 19, 1977. 
You note the requirements for filing statements pursuant to 
Government Code Sections 87203 and 87204, and you ask if it is 
permissable to file one statement. 
You may file one statement covering the period May 5, 1976 
through January 19, 1977, and this statement should be filed 
within 30 days after you leave office. 
You will not need to 
file any additional 
statements. 
When you file your statement, you should advise the filing 
officer of the extended period covered by your statement 
(January 1,1977 through January 19, 1977). 
You may wish to 
advise the filing officer that your one statement fulfills the 
filing requirements of Government Code Sections 87203 and 
87204. 
If you have any questions regarding this procedure, please 
feel free to contact me. 
Sincerely, 
~~OOd 
Program Coordinator 
JCG:lh 
SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 
• 
LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196 


RAYMOND L. SiLIVAN 
ASSOCIATE JUSTICE 
STATE BUILDING 
SAN FRANCISCO 94102

sections.question: You note the requirements for filing statements pursuant to Government Code Sections 87203 and 87204, and you ask if it is permissable to file one statement.
sections.conclusion: You may file one statement covering the period May 5, 1976 through January 19, 1977, and this statement should be filed within 30 days after you leave office. You will not need to file any additional statements. When you file your statement, you should advise the filing officer of the extended period covered by your statement (January 1, 1977 through January 19, 1977).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an Associate Justice of the California Supreme Court retiring mid-term may file a single consolidated Statement of Economic Interests covering the partial year period rather than separate statements as required by Government Code Sections 87203 and 87204.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that Justice Sullivan may file one consolidated Statement of Economic Interests covering May 5, 1976 through January 19, 1977, to be filed within 30 days of leaving office. No additional statements will be required if the filing officer is notified of the extended period covered.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains clear Q&A structure with implicit question and explicit conclusion. Addressed to sitting Supreme Court Justice. References Government Code sections 87203 and 87204. Some OCR artifacts present but content is legible.

QUESTION: You note the requirements for filing statements pursuant to Government Code Sections 87203 and 87204, and you ask if it is permissable to file one statement.

CONCLUSION: You may file one statement covering the period May 5, 1976 through January 19, 1977, and this statement should be filed within 30 days after you leave office. You will not need to file any additional statements. When you file your statement, you should advise the filing officer of the extended period covered by your statement (January 1, 1977 through January 19, 1977).

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Justice Raymond L. Sullivan's inquiry regarding the filing of his Statement of Economic Interests upon his retirement from the California Supreme Court on January 19, 1977, advising that a single consolidated statement covering the period May 5, 1976 through January 19, 1977 satisfies all filing requirements.
